title: refactor: Implement task-based execution for instance AI and phased planning (no-changelog)
author: OlegIvaniv

notes.outcome_clarity: PRESENT. The core issue is very clear: abort signal was passed in wrong position (input object vs config argument). The fix and its rationale are explicit. Two developers would understand the problem and solution. Minor ambiguity: doesn't detail what 'stopping a workflow execution' entails mechanically.
notes.error_states: ABSENT. No mention of error handling, edge cases, or failure modes. What happens if signal is undefined? What if chain.invoke() rejects the config object? No timeout or retry behavior described.
notes.scope_boundaries: PRESENT. Clearly in-scope: fix abort signal handling in ChainLLM node, update tests. Clearly out-of-scope: other chain types, other signal handling patterns. One minor gap: unclear if this affects only ChainLLM or other nodes too.
notes.acceptance_criteria: PARTIALLY PRESENT. States 'tests updated to verify the signal is passed in the correct position' but doesn't specify test cases, expected behavior, or how to verify abort actually stops requests. No explicit pass/fail criteria provided.
notes.data_contracts: PRESENT. Data shapes are specified: input should be { query, signal }, config should be { signal }. The change shows what was wrong and what's right. However, doesn't detail what other config fields might exist or their types.
notes.dependency_context: PARTIALLY PRESENT. References 'ChainLLM node', 'chain.invoke()', and 'LangChain' library behavior. Missing: specific file paths, function signatures, or which other modules depend on this behavior. Linear ticket referenced but not detailed.
notes.behavioral_specificity: PRESENT. Specific detail: 'signal must be passed as the second argument (config)' and 'LangChain ignores signals bundled in the input'. However, vague on abort mechanics: what does 'respect it' mean exactly? Does it cancel pending requests? Kill them mid-stream?
notes.change_type_reasoning: Clearly a bugfix—fixing incorrect signal passing that breaks workflow abort functionality.

data_contracts_notes: Mentions ZIP structure (central directory, local file headers, DEFLATE format) but no schema/contract definitions for data being imported/exported
outcome_clarity_notes: End state is clear: fix entity import by replacing fflate.Unzip with custom central-directory-based parser. Technical approach is explicit. However, ambiguity remains: will this also fix export (self-inclusion)? What is the behavior difference from user perspective?
error_states_notes: Root causes are well documented. Partially present: mentions 'unexpected EOF' symptom and truncation mechanism, but lacks specifics on: what happens if central directory is corrupted? Malformed entries? Timeout behavior? Recovery strategy?
scope_boundaries_notes: Mostly clear: function is decompressFolder, behavior is skipping source archive path. Ambiguity: does this fix apply to all entity imports or specific scenarios? Does export behavior change? Are there other ZIP-related functions affected?
acceptance_criteria_notes: Mostly absent. No testable criteria provided. Implies the fix should: (1) not fail on large archives, (2) not truncate mid-read, (3) handle self-referential entries. But no Given/When/Then or specific test cases. Test checkbox is unchecked.
behavioral_specificity_notes: Technical specificity is high (fflate.Unzip → custom parser, central directory reading, positioned reads, constant memory). But missing: what file size triggers this? What is 'large'? Memory threshold? Processing speed expectations?
dependency_context_notes: Partially present: names fflate.Unzip, fflate.Zip, Node zlib, decompressFolder function. Unclear: exact file paths? Where does import:entities entry point? What calls decompressFolder? Integration points beyond the fix location?

acceptance_criteria_notes: What stays and what's removed are clearly listed, but no testable pass/fail criteria or expected behavior validations are provided. No explicit test cases or validation steps described.
data_contracts_notes: Some data shape changes mentioned (telemetry event structure, feature flag removal from DTOs), but incomplete. No schema, field-level detail, or request/response contract specifics provided.
dependency_context_notes: Good coverage of modules/components affected (frontend, API DTOs, CLI, backend interfaces, graph nodes, LangSmith traces). Specific node names mentioned (discovery, planner, responder, supervisor, process_operations). Some file paths or function signatures would strengthen this.
behavioral_specificity_notes: Specific about what's removed and what's retained. Clear on routing behavior for plan mode (web fetch approvals, plan modify/reject). Vague on how code builder becomes 'only path' — what does execution actually do? No thresholds, timing, or concrete interaction examples.
additional_observations: Strong scope definition and change inventory. Weak on error handling (what if plan mode requests fail? how does supervisor-less routing degrade?). Acceptance criteria assume reviewer knowledge of the system; no independent test strategy provided. Telemetry preservation is concrete, but follow-up work section creates ambiguity about what's truly in scope.
title: refactor(core): Remove multi-agent architecture entry point from AI workflow builder (no-changelog)
author: burivuhster

data_contracts_notes: Partial: sequence diagram shows error response structure (isError, content with GATEWAY_CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED), confirmation payload shape (inputType, resourceDecision with resource/description/options), and POST body shape (approved, resourceDecisionToken). Missing: exact field names, type definitions, optional vs required fields, error response schema details, what fields are in agent snapshot, exact format of decision id and token.
outcome_clarity_notes: Present: High-level goal is clear (implement tool call confirmation flow with HITL protection). Partially ambiguous: exact UI behavior beyond 'show GatewayResourceDecision panel', how agent resumption works mechanically, what 'apply decision' means on the daemon side, error handling paths.
error_states_notes: Partial: One error path shown (GATEWAY_CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED). Missing: what if token validation fails? What if agent snapshot is corrupted/expired? What if user denies? Timeout behavior? Network failure mid-flow? Invalid decision id format?
scope_boundaries_notes: Present: Clear that this affects Computer Use tool calls in instance AI only, applies when confirmationMode: instance. Partially absent: does this apply to all tool types or only certain ones? Does it affect non-Computer-Use tools? What about batch tool calls? Does it change the LocalGateway daemon behavior or only the n8n server behavior?
acceptance_criteria_notes: Absent: No explicit pass/fail criteria. No Given/When/Then scenarios. No test cases listed. Implied: 'tool execute succeeds after confirmation with token' and 'tool execute fails without token', but not stated as acceptance criteria. No metrics for success (e.g., security validation, user experience).
dependency_context_notes: Present: References LocalGateway (Computer Use), fs-proxy Daemon, Mastra framework, n8n Server, Database, Browser frontend, SSE events. Partially absent: exact file paths, module names, which existing code handles suspension/resumption, how Mastra integrates with this flow, database schema changes needed.
